warm_up.question: What city do you live in?
Which room in your home feels the safest if you need to get away quickly?

Still in a relationship.question: Which form(s) of abuse are you experiencing?
Are you recording the incidents (dates, times, details)?
Name one person you can call immediately in an emergency.

Planning to leave.question: When do you plan to leave?
Do you have a go-bag packed with essentials?
Where do you keep emergency cash or cards?
Have you identified a safe place to stay?

Post-separation.question: Has the abuser continued any contact?
Do you have a protection order? If yes, which type?

field.question: Enter the type of legal order:

Post-separation.question: Are you using any safety tools (cameras, door alarms)?

follow_up.question: Which type of support would help most right now?

situation.Still in a relationship: Keep this plan updated, and remember you can always reach out if things escalate.
situation.Planning to leave: Start packing small items now so you're ready when you decide to leave.
situation.Post-separation: It's normal to feel unsettled, refer back to this plan whenever you need a safety reminder.

tips.city: Save the local support numbers for {city} in your phone's speed-dial so you can call immediately if you need help.
tips.safe_room: Keep your essentials (phone, keys, and a small flashlight) in {safe_room} so you can grab them instantly when you need to leave.

living_situation.Family: Let those you live with: {living_situation} know your safe room and cue-word so they can support you in an emergency.
living_situation.Roommates: Let those you live with: {living_situation} know your safe room and cue-word so they can support you in an emergency.
living_situation.Alone: Since you live alone, identify a trusted neighbor or friend and share your safe room location with them.
living_situation.Partner: Because you live with your partner, keep your safety plan hidden (in {safe_room}), and consider storing a digital copy in a password-protected note they won't see.

children.Yes: Practice your safety plan with your children so that when they hear {code_word}, they know exactly what to do.
children.No: Even without children, it helps to identify a second safe space in your home.

tips.code_word: Your code word '{code_word}' should be something natural that won't raise suspicion when used in conversation.
tips.abuse_types: If you face {abuse_types} abuse, remember you don't have to face it alone. Reach out to your emergency contact right away.

recording_incidents.Yes: Log each incident (date, time, and a brief note) so you have clear records if needed later.
recording_incidents.No: Even brief notes on your phone can be helpful. Start logging today.

tips.emergency_contact: Save {emergency_contact} in your phone under a code-word so you can quietly call them when you need help.
tips.leave_timing: Set a real target {leave_timing} and pack one essential item each day until you're ready.

go_bag.Yes: Great! Keep your go-bag in a hidden spot so you can grab it quickly.
go_bag.No: Pack a simple go-bag today with essentials like your ID, some cash, and a charger.

tips.money_location: Stash some cash and a spare card in {money_location}, a spot your partner won't suspect.

safe_place.Yes: Make sure {temp_shelter} knows you may arrive unexpectedly and how to contact you.
safe_place.No: Reach out to Victim Services Toronto or a trusted friend now to secure a shelter option before you leave.

tips.temp_shelter: Keep contact information for {temp_shelter} saved in a secure location and let them know you may need their help.

continued_contact.Yes: Since you've heard from them, stay vigilant and keep your protection order handy if you have one.
continued_contact.No: Even without contact, keep your doors locked and your phone on in case you need to call 911.

protection_order.Yes: Carry your {legal_order} at all times and share copies with anyone who needs to know.
protection_order.No: Consider applying for a protection order; it can help deter unwanted contact.

tips.legal_order: Make sure your {legal_order} is easily accessible and that key people in your life know about its terms.

safety_tools.Yes: Make sure your {equipment} is in working order and placed where you'll notice it.
safety_tools.No: Consider installing a simple door alarm or camera to alert you to unexpected entry.

tips.equipment: Regularly check that your {equipment} is functioning properly and positioned for maximum effectiveness.
tips.support_choice: Reach out to {support_choice} - they can offer the specialized help you need right now.
